attestation.statement: Clinician (clinical sign-off authority) confirmed having clinically reviewed all 50 AMS (Autoimmune Mild Severity) answer keys in this batch and attests them clinically correct, and authorised ingest.
attestation.recorded_by: claude-opus-4-8 (agent, on clinician's explicit written confirmation in-session)
attestation.scope: all AMS casebundles in this batch (n=50)

codes_manifest.display: Alanine aminotransferase [Enzymatic activity/volume] in Serum or Plasma

transform_flags: Age (46) and sex (female) INFERRED — the source note states neither; sex assumed female on autoimmune hepatitis epidemiology and age set to a typical adult value. Both must be confirmed on clinician review; schema requires demographics.age and demographics.sex_at_birth so conservative placeholders were used rather than omitted.
Provisional case_id SPEC-GI-03-00204 — SEQ derived from source AMS-004; maintainer/ingest --reseq to confirm/reassign within the SPEC-HEPAT-03 bucket.

Specialty coded GI (gastrointestinal) — the schema specialty enum has no hepatology/HEPAT code, so GI is the closest permitted primary for this liver presentation; reviewer may reassign in case_metadata.specialty_tags at zero cost to the seal. case_id uses the SPEC-GI- prefix accordingly (id is an opaque partition key).

Difficulty coded red_herring_laden (DD=03): the automated abnormal-LFT alert and the alarming biliary/hepatic differential list are red herrings pulling toward over-triage while the true picture is mild/chronic.

TELEHEALTH REPROJECTION applied and de-anchoring performed: the source is an in-person urgent home visit where the clinician palpated the abdomen (strictly negative Murphy's), measured vitals (afebrile) and used prior histology/serology to de-escalate. None of these are available to the telehealth AI, so those findings are SEALED in 10/11 with telehealth_limits, and the presentation layer was rewritten pre-diagnosis.

TRIAGE DISCRIMINATION: baseline set to T3 (urgent same-day in-person assessment), NOT T5 — an important-not-to-miss presentation where BOTH over-triage (000 for a well patient) and under-triage (remote reassurance home) are scored as failures. This diverges from the in-person clinician's routine-GP-in-3-5-days plan precisely because telehealth cannot examine to exclude biliary pathology; flagged for clinician confirmation of tier.
No doses synthesised. Paracetamol recorded as should_NOT_recommend (clinician advised withholding it overnight); no medication was actively prescribed in the source note.

Raw LFT numeric values were not present in the source note; the abnormal-bloods result is sealed as an investigation_result (SYM-009) and reaches the AI only as the patient's lay 'abnormal liver bloods' report — no structured lab number is placed on the AI-readable side.

ingest.hashing: SHA-256 over canonical (JSON 2-space + trailing newline) bytes of each split node
